title: The Starting Signal at the Loss Node
concept: The backward pass begins at the loss node, where the model compares prediction to target and creates the first correction signal that tells the network which direction would reduce the error.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What can a tiny local slope do to a backward signal?

options.text: It can weaken the final gradient for earlier weights.
It instantly shuffles the training data.
It removes the loss function from the model.

exercises.explain: Correct! A weak local slope can shrink the correction signal as it travels backward.

cards.title: The alarm starts at the sensor
cards.body: In a school fire alarm, the first signal does not begin in the hallway speakers. It begins at the smoke sensor that noticed something was wrong. In training, the first correction signal also begins where the mistake is noticed: the loss node.

cards.title: The first correction signal
cards.body: The loss node compares the model's prediction with the true target. From that comparison, it produces the first backward message: if the prediction were nudged a little, would the loss go up or down? That starting message launches backpropagation.

cards.title: A GPS saying "too far east"
cards.body: A GPS correction does not just say, "You are wrong." It says which way to move: west, east, north, or south. The loss node behaves similarly. It sends a directional correction that tells the network how the prediction should shift to reduce the mistake.

cards.title: Prediction too high vs. too low
cards.body: If the true answer should be 1.0 but the model predicts 0.2, the loss sends a signal saying the prediction needs to move upward. If the true answer should be 0.0 but the model predicts 0.9, the loss sends the opposite message: push downward. That first sign matters before the signal reaches earlier layers.

cards.title: Loss is more than a report card
cards.body: It is easy to think the loss is just a scoreboard printed at the end. But during training, the loss is also the launch pad for correction. It does not merely announce the mistake; it starts the backward signal that can fix it.

cards.title: Two possible starting directions
cards.body: What the loss can tell the model first:

left.text:
Loss says: raise the prediction.
Backward signal starts upward.

right.text:
Loss says: lower the prediction.
Backward signal starts downward.

exercises.prompt: Why does the backward pass begin at the loss node?

options.text: Because that is where prediction and target are compared, creating the first correction signal.
Because the input layer cannot store numbers.
Because the loss node chooses the next dataset batch.
Because gradients are only useful after testing ends.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The loss node is where the model first learns how the prediction should change.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The starting signal from the loss can tell the network whether the prediction should move up or down to reduce error.
exercises.explain: True! The first backward signal includes direction, not just the size of the mistake.

exercises.sentence: The backward pass starts at the ___ node because that is where error is measured.
exercises.explain: Correct. The loss node compares prediction against target and launches the correction signal.

exercises.prompt: Which case best shows the starting signal from the loss node?

options.text: The true label is 0, the model predicts 0.9, and the loss sends a "push lower" signal backward.
The model guesses a label and never computes error.
The input layer changes the labels before prediction.

exercises.explain: Correct! The loss node starts backprop by telling the network how the prediction should move.

exercises.prompt: A binary classifier predicts 0.92 for an example whose true label is 0. What is the most useful way to describe the very first signal that leaves the loss node?

options.text: It says the prediction is too high and should be pushed downward, then that correction begins traveling backward through the network.
It says the model should ignore the example because the guess was confident.
It resets every weight to zero before learning starts.
It sends the original input image backward instead of an error signal.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The loss node compares 0.92 to the true target 0 and launches a downward correction signal. Next, we will step back and see why this whole idea changed neural network history.
